iti93:1.1: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti93:2.3: The fire of passion, the fire of aversion, the fire of delusion.
iti93:3.1: The fire of passion burns in a mortal
iti93:3.2: delighting in, smitten with sensual desires;
iti93:3.3: the fire of aversion, in a malevolent person
iti93:4.1: the fire of delusion, in a bewildered person
iti93:4.4: –fond of self-identity– unreleased from Māra’s shackles,
iti93:5.2: the wombs of common animals, demons,
iti93:5.3: the realm of the hungry ghosts.
iti93:6.2: are devoted to the message of the rightly self-awakened,
iti93:6.3: put out the fire of passion,
iti93:6.4:
constantly perceiving the foul.
They, superlative people,
iti93:7.1: put out the fire of aversion with good will,
iti93:7.3: and the fire of delusion with the discernment leading
iti93:8.1: They, the masterful, untiring by night & day,
iti93:9.1: They, the wise, with an attainer-of-wisdom’s noble vision,
iti93:9.3: directly knowing the ending of birth,
